title: Pentacalia carinata
intro: Pentacalia carinata is a plant species belonging to the family Asteraceae, commonly known as a member of the daisy or sunflower family. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help to alleviate symptoms of infections, wounds, and other conditions. The bioactive constituents of Pentacalia carinata include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and sesquiterpenes, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal preparations, Pentacalia carinata is often used to make infusions, teas, and tinctures, which are used to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and respiratory infections.

This family is further divided into various subfamilies and tribes, with Pentacalia carinata belonging to the tribe Senecioneae. The genus Pentacalia, to which Pentacalia carinata belongs, is a group of plants native to the tropical regions of Central and South America.
